03 / 06 — Case studies

Four projects. Each one ships.

01 / 04

LexTrack AI

AI/ML Product Engineering Intern · Capstone PM Lead

Sep 2025 — Present

  • TypeScript
  • RAG
  • Gemini
  • Prompt Eng
  • Microservices

The headline

Restaurant onboarding from 30 minutes to 5.

The problem

SMB restaurants needed SevenRooms-grade reservation systems but couldn't afford them, and existing no-code tools required 30+ minutes of manual metadata config per merchant — killing time-to-value before users even saw the product.

The build

Architected a 0→1 reservation template inside a 1-click no-code platform. Hybrid AI architecture: deterministic rules + Gemini LLM for the ambiguous cases. 80+ metadata fields across 11 categories, generated from a single prompt. 3-layer fallback ensured the LLM never blocked critical paths.

The impact

  • →80% onboarding time cut (30 min → 5 min)
  • →70% LLM cost reduction via caching + intelligent fallback
  • →99.5% uptime across 50+ active restaurant partners
  • →85% weekly retention, 4.2/5 CSAT in 8 weeks
  • →$2M+ TAM identified through 25+ user interviews
Product demoWatch the product demo↗

02 / 04

QuadTax

Founder & Lead Engineer

Mar 2026 — Present

  • Next.js
  • FastAPI
  • OpenAI
  • Pydantic
  • Tesseract OCR

The headline

AI tax filing for 2M+ nonresident aliens in the US.

The problem

F-1/J-1 visa holders face the same tax filing nightmare every April: confusing IRS rules, FICA tax illegally withheld by employers (IRC § 3121(b)(19)), and a market underserved by TurboTax. Manual prep takes 35+ minutes per return and the math errors are common.

The build

Hybrid execution engine. LLM agents handle reasoning (residency determination, treaty evaluation, document parsing) while pure Python handles the math (tax brackets, regulatory lookups). 100% mathematical accuracy guaranteed because the deterministic zone never sees the LLM. OCR pipeline (Tesseract + pdfplumber) extracts W-2, 1042-S, I-94 data automatically. Generates official IRS forms (1040-NR, 8843, 8833) as PDFs.

The impact

  • →35 min → <10 min total prep time
  • →90% reduction in manual data entry
  • →100% math accuracy via deterministic execution zone
  • →FICA recovery module identifies illegal withholding
Source codeCheck out the GitHub repo↗

03 / 04

AI Purchase Analytics

Personal Project

Oct 2025

  • Python
  • Pandas
  • Gemini
  • Streamlit
  • Plotly

The headline

Executive reporting from 4 hours to 5 minutes.

The problem

Retail leadership teams burn entire workdays pulling RFM segments, churn risk scores, and cohort retention by hand in Excel. By the time the deck is ready, the data is stale.

The build

Automated analytics platform. Pandas does the heavy lift on RFM segmentation and cohort retention. Gemini API generates strategic insights and revenue opportunities from the structured output. Streamlit delivers it as an interactive dashboard non-technical execs can actually use.

The impact

  • →95% reporting time reduction (4 hr → 5 min)
  • →110K+ transactions processed
  • →96K+ customer profiles served
  • →Churn prediction AUC 0.82 — 15% improvement in retention targeting
Source codeCheck out the GitHub repo↗

04 / 04

Squirrel AI

Product Vision & Lead Developer

2025

  • React
  • FastAPI
  • OpenAI
  • Embeddings

The headline

Plain-English explanations for any GitHub repo.

The problem

Onboarding to a new codebase is the worst part of any engineering job. README files lie, code comments are stale, and ramp-up time bleeds productivity for weeks.

The build

AI-powered repo analyzer. Pulls any public GitHub repo, extracts code structure (functions, classes, imports across 11 languages), generates a project summary, and lets users ask context-aware questions with file references in the answers.

The impact

  • →Multi-language support (Python, JS, TS, Go, Java, +)
  • →Context-aware Q&A with cited file references
  • →Cached repo analysis for sub-second subsequent queries
Source codeCheck out the GitHub repo↗

B — Chess

Daily practice. Endgames teach you to ship.

chess.com · current

0

peak

0

Position after 5...a6

♜♞♝♛♚♝♜♟♟♟♟♟♟♟♞♘♙♘♙♙♙♙♙♙♖♗♕♔♗♖

1. e4 c5   2. Nf3 d6   3. d4 cxd4   4. Nxd4 Nf6   5. Nc3 a6

Favorite opening — Sicilian, Najdorf Variation

The most-played defense to 1. e4 at the elite level. Black plays …a6 on move five — a quiet pawn push that doesn't develop a piece, doesn't capture anything, doesn't attack.

But it sets up everything. It controls b5, prepares …e5 or …e6, and refuses to commit to a bishop placement until White shows their hand. It's the chess equivalent of shipping the simplest possible interface and letting requirements emerge from contact with users.

Fischer played it. Kasparov played it. I'm 1272 — but I'm learning.

C — Compounding product sense

and counting
